feedback.text: Dear Mr/Ms, I would like to express my serious concerns with this letter about the lack of consumer protection in the digital game industry. Digital video games, currently sold exclusively on online platforms, are currently outside key regulations, which regularly penalises consumers. It is increasingly common for large publishers to withdraw access to purchased games unilaterally and without a clear reason for multinational companies with significant profits. This is often done with reference to provisions in the User Agreement (EULA), which state that access can be terminated at any time and without justification. This is an unsustainable situation for consumers. Personally, I have experienced how frustrating this can be. A game for which I paid for and in which I invested many hours was removed from the platform without warning. My access was permanently lost, without compensation or possibility of objection. I therefore urge you to explicitly include digital video games in the Digital Fairness Act. As with other digital products, there should be clear, enforceable rights for consumers to be able to rely on the sustainability of a digital purchase. With the emergence of more and more fully digital distribution models, it is very important that legislation is growing and that consumers are not left behind. Thank you in advance for your attention and commitment.
